events.text:
I reviewed the lookahead schedule image. Key finding: Activity D's (Framing) printed duration label is illegible, but since the bars are drawn early-start and to scale, D's duration can be read off the chart geometry directly — it runs from workday 10 (end of the Slab cure lag) to workday 14 (start of Roof dry-in), i.e. 4 workdays.

D sits on a single, non-branching chain with zero float, and the critical path continues through HVAC rough (H) into the tied Trim/Flooring finish and Final inspection. So the disruption (D needs 2 extra workdays) is a direct 1:1 hit to the project: baseline finish at workday 42 shifts to workday 44 — a 2-workday delay, with no compressible cure lag or inspection hold available to absorb it.
